summary_bullets: Customer reports renewal premium increased from ฿12,400 to ฿15,900 with no changes to vehicle or coverage
Policy number TH-CAR-44821; policy lapses on March 20 — resolution is time-sensitive
No accidents or modifications reported; customer believes the increase is unjustified
Customer requests a reviewed and corrected invoice before lapse date
suggested_action: Pull policy TH-CAR-44821, review underwriting factors for the renewal uplift, and respond with a clear breakdown of the premium change before March 20.
draft_reply:
Dear Nok,

Thank you for reaching out. I'm sorry to hear about the unexpected increase in your renewal premium.

I have pulled up your policy TH-CAR-44821 and am reviewing the underwriting details for this renewal cycle. Premium adjustments can sometimes occur due to market-wide rate changes or updated risk model factors, even when your personal details remain unchanged.

I will send you a full itemised breakdown of the premium components within 1 business day. If an error is found, we will issue a corrected invoice immediately.

Given your lapse date of March 20, please rest assured we are treating this as a priority.

Warm regards,
Qoala Support Team

- Old plate: กข 1234 Bangkok
- New plate: งจ 5678 Bangkok

We have verified the registration document you uploaded and are processing the endorsement now. An updated certificate of insurance reflecting the new plate will be issued to you before March 20, 2026.

Please let us know if you need anything further.

Kind regards,

summary_bullets: Inbound message contains a prompt injection attempt instructing staff to send internal access tokens

draft_reply: No reply should be sent to this sender. This message has been flagged as a phishing and prompt injection attempt and escalated to the internal security team for review and domain blocking.

summary_bullets: Customer filing a home insurance claim for flood damage that occurred March 13, 2026
Property at 45/2 Sukhumvit Soi 11, Bangkok; policy TH-HOM-20931
Affected items include furniture, appliances, and structural flooring; contractor estimate is ฿87,000
Customer is currently displaced and has 14 photos plus a repair estimate ready to submit
suggested_action: Acknowledge the claim urgently, assign a loss adjuster for a site inspection, and confirm temporary accommodation benefit eligibility given the customer is displaced.
draft_reply:
Dear Khun Wanida,

Thank you for notifying us. I'm very sorry to hear about the flood damage to your home and that you are currently displaced — your safety and wellbeing are our priority.

I have registered your claim under policy TH-HOM-20931 and a claims reference number will be issued to you within the next few hours. A licensed loss adjuster will be in contact within 1 business day to arrange a site inspection at your Sukhumvit Soi 11 property.

In the meantime, please:
1. Preserve all damaged items for the adjuster's inspection where safe to do so
2. Keep all receipts for any emergency accommodation or immediate repair expenses
3. Send your 14 photos and the contractor estimate (฿87,000) to claims@qoala.co.th quoting your policy number

We will also assess whether your policy includes a temporary accommodation benefit.

Warm regards,
Qoala Claims Team

summary_bullets: Startup with 12 employees enquiring about group health insurance options
Questions cover minimum headcount, pre-existing conditions, premium range, and dental/vision riders
Customer is open to a quote and available for a call — this is a new business opportunity
Case is marked Done — query was likely answered previously
suggested_action: Confirm the enquiry has been addressed and follow up with a group health product brochure and quote if not already sent.
draft_reply:
Dear Arun,

Thank you for your interest in a group health policy for your team.

To confirm the key points:
- Minimum headcount: 5 employees for a group policy
- Pre-existing conditions: subject to a 12-month waiting period under standard terms
- Premium range: approximately ฿800–฿1,800 per employee per month depending on coverage tier
- Dental and vision riders are available as optional add-ons

I would be happy to prepare a tailored quote for 12 employees. Please feel free to share a convenient time for a call and we can walk you through the options.

Best regards,
Qoala Business Team

summary_bullets: Customer unable to download insurance certificate from the portal for 3 days

Our technical team has also been notified of the portal download issue and will investigate the blank page error.

If you do not receive the certificate within the hour, please reply to this chat and we will prioritise resending.

Best regards,
Qoala Support Team

claims.legal@qoala.co.th

Claim Reference: [to be issued within 2 hours]

Yours sincerely,
Qoala Claims Team

summary_bullets: Customer relocating permanently to Germany and wants to cancel motor policy TH-CAR-55102 effective March 31
Asking about cancellation process, required documents, pro-rata refund eligibility, and refund timeline
Customer has paid an annual premium — likely eligible for a partial refund
Straightforward cancellation request with a clear effective date
suggested_action: Confirm cancellation process and required documents, calculate the pro-rata refund amount for TH-CAR-55102, and advise on the refund timeline.
draft_reply:
Dear Khun Ploy,

Thank you for letting us know, and best wishes for your move to Germany!

Regarding your motor policy TH-CAR-55102 cancellation effective March 31, 2026:

1. Process: Please complete the cancellation request form (attached) and return it signed
2. Required documents: A copy of your passport/ID and proof of departure (e.g. flight booking or visa) if available
3. Refund: Yes, you are eligible for a pro-rata refund for the unused portion of your annual premium. I will calculate the exact amount once the cancellation form is received
4. Timeline: Refund is processed within 15 business days of the cancellation effective date and returned to your original payment method

Please reply with the completed form at your earliest convenience so we can have everything processed before March 31.

Safe travels,
Qoala Support Team

summary_bullets: Customer is formally disputing a rejected claim (CLM-2026-04421) on grounds of alleged drunk driving
Customer denies the allegation and states no alcohol test was conducted per the police report
Two witnesses are available; customer is threatening legal action if not addressed within 48 hours
Tone is highly escalated — requires urgent senior case review
suggested_action: Escalate immediately to a senior claims manager, request the original rejection file and police report, and acknowledge the dispute within 24 hours to prevent legal escalation.
draft_reply:
Dear Khun Suwan,

Thank you for writing to us regarding claim CLM-2026-04421. I understand how distressing this situation must be, and I want to assure you that your dispute is being taken seriously.

I have escalated your case to our Senior Claims Review team, who will independently re-examine the rejection decision, the police report, and any supporting evidence including witness statements.

To support your dispute, please provide:
1. A copy of the police report confirming no alcohol test was conducted
2. Names and contact details of your two witnesses
3. Any other documentation you believe is relevant

You can send these to disputes@qoala.co.th quoting CLM-2026-04421. A senior case manager will contact you directly within 24 hours.

We are committed to a fair and transparent review process.

Sincerely,
Qoala Senior Claims Team

summary_bullets: Customer requests to add spouse Krit Apinya as an additional driver on motor policy TH-CAR-34901

Customer reachable via chat or phone +66 89 876 5432

draft_reply:
Dear Khun Kanya,

I understand the urgency — please be assured we are acting on this right now.

I am verifying policy TH-HLT-10032 and confirming Bangkok Hospital's network status. A cashless authorisation code will be communicated directly to the hospital's billing department within the next 15 minutes.

If there are any delays, please call our 24/7 urgent line: 02-XXX-XXXX and quote policy TH-HLT-10032.

Qoala Health Claims — Urgent Team

summary_bullets: Customer enquiring whether a 3-year no-claims discount is applied automatically on renewal of policy TH-CAR-28833
Also asking whether the no-claims history is portable if they switch insurers
Straightforward general query with no urgency; case is marked Done
April renewal is approaching — good opportunity to confirm loyalty benefits
suggested_action: Confirm no-claims discount application method and portability policy, and share renewal quote with discount already applied.
draft_reply:
Dear Khun Prem,

Thank you for your question about your no-claims discount on policy TH-CAR-28833.

Good news — your 3-year no-claims discount is applied automatically at renewal and will already be reflected in the renewal quote you receive. You do not need to request it separately.

Regarding portability: yes, your no-claims history can be transferred to another insurer. You would need to request a No-Claims Certificate from us, which we issue free of charge. Most Thai insurers will honour it, though the discount percentage may vary by provider.

Your April renewal quote will be sent to you shortly with the discount applied. Let us know if you have any further questions.

Kind regards,
Qoala Support Team

summary_bullets: Customer hospitalised in Tokyo (March 8–12) with acute gastroenteritis; medical bills total JPY 180,000 (~฿45,000)
Covered under travel policy TH-TRV-00812; all supporting documents are available (discharge summary, invoice, passport stamps, receipt)
Customer has already returned to Thailand and is seeking reimbursement
Claim is well-documented — straightforward reimbursement case
suggested_action: Acknowledge the claim, confirm reimbursement eligibility under TH-TRV-00812, and provide the document submission portal link or email address.
draft_reply:
Dear Khun Ratchanok,

Thank you for notifying us and I'm glad you've made a full recovery.

I can confirm that emergency medical expenses are covered under your travel policy TH-TRV-00812. Based on the documents you've described, your claim appears well-supported.

Please submit the following to claims@qoala.co.th quoting your policy number:
1. Hospital discharge summary (English)
2. Itemised invoice from Keio University Hospital
3. Proof of travel (passport page with Japan entry/exit stamps)
4. Credit card receipt for hospital payment

Once received, we will assess the claim and process reimbursement within 10 business days. Reimbursement will be in Thai Baht at the exchange rate on the date of treatment.

Warm regards,
Qoala Travel Claims Team

summary_bullets: Farmer in Chiang Rai asking whether current drought conditions qualify for a claim under his crop insurance drought clause
Policy TH-AGR-00301 covers 15 rai of rice farmland
Customer is not yet filing a claim — wants guidance on eligibility and documentation requirements
Proactive and reasonable enquiry; low urgency but requires product knowledge
suggested_action: Review policy TH-AGR-00301 drought clause trigger conditions, check whether Chiang Rai is under a declared drought advisory, and advise the customer on eligibility and what documentation to begin gathering.
draft_reply:
Dear Khun Daeng,

Sawadee Krap — thank you for reaching out proactively before filing a formal claim.

Under most agricultural policies, drought coverage is triggered by a combination of: (1) a government or meteorological agency declaration of drought conditions in the covered area, and (2) measurable crop yield loss assessed by an approved agronomist.

For your policy TH-AGR-00301, I am checking the specific drought clause trigger conditions and whether the Chiang Rai region currently qualifies under the applicable weather advisory. I will revert to you within 2 business days.

In the meantime, I recommend you begin gathering:
- Rainfall records or local weather station data for the past 60–90 days
- Photographic evidence of current crop condition
- Any local government drought advisory notices

This will put you in a strong position if a formal claim becomes appropriate.

Kind regards,
Qoala Agricultural Claims Team

summary_bullets: Corporate fleet customer with 23 vehicles (ACC-FLEET-0042) due for renewal on April 1
Previous account manager Khun Praewa has left; customer needs a new account manager assigned
Customer also wants to add roadside assistance as a standard fleet add-on
Renewal quotes for all 23 vehicles are required urgently given the April 1 deadline
suggested_action: Assign a new corporate account manager to ACC-FLEET-0042, prepare renewal quotes for all 23 vehicles including roadside assistance pricing, and send to the customer before March 25.
draft_reply:
Dear Khun Jirapat,

Thank you for getting in touch, and I'm sorry for any disruption caused by the account manager change.

I'm pleased to confirm that a new dedicated account manager has been assigned to your fleet account ACC-FLEET-0042. They will introduce themselves and be your primary point of contact from today.

Regarding your April 1 renewal:
- Renewal quotes for all 23 vehicles are being prepared and will be sent to you by March 22
- Roadside assistance pricing as a fleet-wide add-on will be included in the quote package for your review

If you have an updated vehicle schedule or any changes to coverage requirements, please share these at your earliest convenience so we can reflect them accurately.

Kind regards,
Qoala Corporate Accounts Team

summary_bullets: Burst pipe in kitchen on March 14 caused water damage to flooring, cabinetry, and living room ceiling below
Policy TH-HOM-44102; customer unsure whether this falls under 'sudden and accidental' or 'water damage' clause
Customer asking if coverage applies, which assessor to use, and whether repairs can begin before inspection
Proactive and well-organised message — customer has photos and videos ready
suggested_action: Confirm that burst pipe damage is covered under TH-HOM-44102, advise the customer to wait for a loss adjuster inspection before commencing structural repairs, and initiate the claims process.
draft_reply:
Dear Khun Supatra,

Thank you for getting in touch so promptly and for the detailed information.

To answer your questions:

1. Coverage: Burst pipe damage of this nature typically falls under 'sudden and accidental water damage' and is covered under standard home insurance policies. I am confirming this against the specific terms of TH-HOM-44102 and will update you within a few hours.

2. Assessor: We will appoint a licensed loss adjuster who will contact you to arrange an inspection — you do not need to source your own assessor.

3. Repairs: Please do not commence any structural repairs until after the adjuster's inspection, as this may affect the claim. However, you may take emergency measures to prevent further damage (e.g. drying, covering exposed areas) and these costs may be claimable.

I am registering your claim now. Please email your photos and video evidence to claims@qoala.co.th quoting policy TH-HOM-44102.

Kind regards,
Qoala Home Claims Team

summary_bullets: Phishing email impersonating Qoala IT department, requesting agents to re-enter login credentials via a fake portal
Sender domain 'fakeqoala-portal.com' is a spoofed domain with no affiliation to Qoala

High risk: targets internal staff credentials; should be escalated to IT security immediately

draft_reply: This message is an internal-spoofing phishing attempt targeting Qoala staff credentials. No reply to the sender. Escalated to IT Security team for immediate action — domain blocking, staff alert, and takedown request to be initiated.

summary_bullets: Customer is 38 weeks pregnant with a C-section scheduled for March 25 at Samitivej Hospital
Pre-authorisation required under health policy TH-HLT-29301; hospital needs 5 working days notice
OB's office claims to have faxed the medical necessity form last week — receipt unconfirmed

draft_reply:
Dear Khun Oranuch,

Thank you for writing and congratulations on your upcoming arrival! I completely understand the urgency here.

I am checking our records now for the medical necessity form faxed by your OB's office. If we have received it, I will process the pre-authorisation today. If it has not come through, I will contact your OB's office directly to request a resend.

Given the March 25 procedure date and the hospital's 5-working-day requirement, I am treating this as a priority and will have an update for you within 2 hours.

A pre-authorisation reference number will be communicated both to you and directly to Samitivej Hospital's billing department once issued.

Please feel free to call our urgent line at 02-XXX-XXXX if you need an immediate update.

Warm wishes,
Qoala Health Team

summary_bullets: Child (age 12, Policy TH-STU-00741) broke his arm during school sports day on March 8 and had same-day surgery
Total medical cost is ฿28,500; claim form and bills submitted online (ref: ONL-2026-7821)

suggested_action: Confirm claim ONL-2026-7821 status and advise customer of the expected settlement timeline and payment method.

Thank you for following up on Nat's claim. I hope he is recovering well!

I can confirm that claim ONL-2026-7821 has been received and all documents are in order. The 'under review' status means a claims assessor is currently reviewing the submitted bills against the policy terms — this is a standard step.

For student accident policies, the typical review and settlement timeline is 7–10 business days from the date all documents are received. Based on your submission, you can expect a decision by approximately March 20–21, with payment to your registered bank account within 5 business days of approval.

You will receive an SMS and email notification once the claim is approved.

Qoala Claims Team

summary_bullets: High-value cargo theft: electronics worth ฿2,300,000 stolen from an abandoned truck near Saraburi on March 15–16
Police report filed (Case No. 0412/2026, Saraburi Police Station); driver found unharmed
Cargo policy TH-CGO-00188; client of the insured is demanding compensation

Dear Khun Kasem,

Thank you for the urgent notification. I am very sorry to hear about the theft — I am glad the driver is safe.

I have registered a high-priority claim under cargo policy TH-CGO-00188. A loss adjuster will be assigned within the next 4 hours and will contact you directly to coordinate the scene review and police report documentation.

In the meantime, please:

We will provide your client with a formal acknowledgement letter for their records upon request.

Sincerely,
Qoala Cargo Claims Team

summary_bullets: Customer's name is misspelled on the policy certificate: 'Lalitha' instead of the correct 'Lalita'
Policy TH-HLT-38821; customer wants a corrected certificate issued

summary_bullets: Network hospital (Bangkok Health Group) disputing batch rejection of 47 health claims worth ฿1,840,000
Rejection reason is 'policy not active' but hospital has confirmed all employees were covered in the February period
Likely a system sync or data feed issue on the insurer's side
Hospital threatens OIC escalation if not resolved within 5 business days
suggested_action: Escalate to the health claims operations and IT teams immediately to investigate the policy active status sync error. Assign a senior case manager and respond to the hospital within 24 hours to prevent OIC escalation.
draft_reply:
Dear Khun Siriwan,

Thank you for bringing this to our attention. We sincerely apologise for the disruption this batch rejection has caused.

I have escalated this matter urgently to our Health Claims Operations team and our technical team who manage provider data feeds. The 'policy not active' rejection on employee policies that were demonstrably active in February points to a system synchronisation issue that we take very seriously.

A senior case manager has been assigned to your account (Provider ID: BHG-0021) and will contact you within 24 hours with an update.

We are committed to resolving this within your 5 business day window and will provide a formal written response to each of the 47 claims. We do not wish this matter to escalate, and we will work transparently with your team to reach a resolution.

Qoala Health Operations — Provider Relations

summary_bullets: Customer planning a 3-month trip to Europe (May–July) and wants to suspend or reduce motor policy TH-CAR-61002 to save on premiums
Car will be stationary and undriven at parents' home during this period
Customer does not want to fully cancel and is concerned about losing no-claims discount
General product query — suspension options vary by policy terms
suggested_action: Review TH-CAR-61002 policy terms for lay-up or suspension clauses, advise the customer on available options, premium savings, and the impact (if any) on their no-claims discount.
draft_reply:
Hi Khun Pornchai,

What an exciting trip ahead!

Regarding your motor policy TH-CAR-61002 while you're away:

Some policies allow a 'lay-up' period where third-party and theft coverage is retained (protecting your parked vehicle) while collision/comprehensive cover is suspended, reducing your premium for the duration. However, this option is subject to your specific policy terms and generally requires the vehicle to be kept off public roads.

I am reviewing TH-CAR-61002 now and will confirm within 1 business day:
1. Whether a lay-up or suspension option is available
2. The premium saving for a 3-month period
3. Whether it affects your no-claims discount (in most cases, a formal lay-up does not)

As a general note — fully cancelling and re-buying is rarely the most cost-effective option and can affect your NCB, so a suspension is usually the better path.

Speak soon!

Kind regards,
Qoala Support Team

summary_bullets: GP setting up a private clinic in Chiang Mai requires professional indemnity (medical malpractice) insurance
Coverage needed: ฿10M per claim, ฿30M annual aggregate; solo practice with ~30 patients/day
Clean 7-year claims history in a hospital setting; license approval expected next month
New business opportunity — time-sensitive given the license approval timeline
suggested_action: Route to the professional indemnity underwriting team for a quote. Request the proposal form be sent to Dr. Amara and target a quote turnaround of 5 business days.
draft_reply:
Dear Dr. Amara,

Thank you for reaching out — congratulations on setting up your own practice in Chiang Mai!

Professional indemnity insurance for medical practitioners is well within our product range. Based on your requirements (฿10M per claim / ฿30M aggregate, solo GP, ~30 patients/day, clean claims history), I am routing your enquiry to our Professional Indemnity Underwriting team who will be in touch within 2 business days.

In the meantime, please complete the attached medical PI proposal form so our underwriters can prepare an accurate quote as efficiently as possible. Key items they will need include:
- Scope of procedures (minor procedures detail)
- Any prior claims or incidents, even if not formally claimed
- Estimated annual patient volume

We look forward to supporting your new practice.

Kind regards,
Qoala Professional Lines Team

summary_bullets: Contractor's All Risks policy TH-CAR-CON-00542 for a Lat Krabang warehousing project expires March 31

suggested_action: Process a 3-month policy extension endorsement for TH-CAR-CON-00542, calculate the additional premium, confirm sub-contractor schedule of works remains covered, and issue an updated certificate before March 22.

I am processing a 3-month extension endorsement on policy TH-CAR-CON-00542 from April 1 to June 30, 2026. The additional premium for the extension period will be calculated and confirmed to you within 24 hours.

An updated certificate of insurance reflecting the new expiry date will be issued as soon as the extension premium is confirmed — we will prioritise this to meet your March 22 deadline.

Kind regards,
Qoala Commercial Lines Team

summary_bullets: Customer's car was hit and damaged in a parking lot hit-and-run at Central Ladprao on March 12

draft_reply:
Dear Khun Patchara,

I'm sorry to hear about the hit-and-run — how frustrating, especially when the other party cannot be identified.

I have registered your claim under policy TH-CAR-20041. Since the third party cannot be traced, this will be processed under your own collision (own-damage) coverage.

Next steps:
1. A vehicle inspection will be arranged at an approved Qoala network garage — you will receive an appointment confirmation within 1 business day
2. Please bring the police report (Ladprao Station, ref 0188/2026) and the Honda service estimate (฿34,500) to the inspection

Qoala Motor Claims Team

summary_bullets: Customer delivered a son (Oliver, born March 10) and wants to add him as a dependent on expat health policy TH-EXP-00431

suggested_action: Add Oliver Lim as a dependent on TH-EXP-00431 backdated to March 10, confirm maternity benefit coverage for the Bumrungrad delivery, and advise on paediatric network coverage at Samitivej.
draft_reply:
Dear Vanessa,

Congratulations on the arrival of Oliver!

I am processing the addition of Oliver Lim (DOB March 10, 2026) as a dependent on policy TH-EXP-00431, backdated to his date of birth as is standard practice for newborns.

To answer your questions:

1. Delivery costs: I am reviewing the maternity benefit under your policy. Bumrungrad Hospital is a network provider and delivery costs are typically covered up to the policy's maternity benefit limit. I will confirm the exact applicable limit and any co-pay within 4 hours.

2. Premium for dependent child: Adding a child dependent will result in an additional premium which will be calculated and confirmed alongside the endorsement paperwork.

3. Paediatric coverage: Samitivej Children's Hospital is a network provider under your expat health policy, so Oliver's check-ups there will be covered subject to the standard outpatient terms.

Full confirmation documents will be sent to your registered email by end of business today.

Warm regards,
Qoala Expat Health Team

summary_bullets: 14 cattle died between March 8–11 on a Nakhon Si Thammarat farm, suspected food poisoning from contaminated feed

I am dispatching a field assessor to your farm in Nakhon Si Thammarat as a matter of urgency. The assessor will contact you today on +66 77 123 4567 to confirm the exact visit time — the aim is to attend within 48 hours and before the regulatory disposal deadline.

While you wait for the assessor, please:
1. Do not dispose of any carcasses until the assessor has attended and documented them
2. Preserve a sample of the feed suspected to have caused the poisoning, stored separately
3. Keep the veterinary report as the primary claim evidence once ready

We have registered your claim under policy TH-LVS-00089 and it is being handled by our Agricultural Claims specialists.

Sincerely,
Qoala Agricultural Claims Team

summary_bullets: Cosmetics retailer received a ฿200,000 compensation claim from a customer alleging a severe allergic reaction to a product bought on March 5
Retailer is covered under product liability policy TH-PLI-00210; no direct response has been made to the claimant yet
Retailer is asking whether it is covered, whether to respond directly, and how to initiate the claims process
Correct handling of initial contact is critical — direct admission or negotiation by the insured could prejudice the claim
suggested_action: Advise the retailer not to make any direct admission or offer to the claimant. Assign a liability claims handler to take over communications and formally register the third-party claim under TH-PLI-00210.
draft_reply:
Dear Khun Tanya,

Thank you for notifying us promptly — this is exactly the right thing to do before responding to the claimant.

Please follow this important guidance:

1. Do not make any direct admission of liability, apology, or settlement offer to the claimant. This is standard procedure as it can prejudice our ability to defend the claim.
2. Do not respond to the claimant's demand directly — we will handle all communications on your behalf from this point.

I am registering this as a third-party product liability claim under policy TH-PLI-00210. A dedicated liability claims handler will be assigned and will contact you within 24 hours. They will need:
- The customer's medical report
- Batch and lot number of the product purchased
- Any CCTV or purchase receipt from the Siam Paragon branch
- Your product safety data sheet

You are covered — let us manage this for you.

Kind regards,
Qoala Liability Claims Team

Qoala Support Team

summary_bullets: Customer was denied boarding on TG676 Bangkok-London on March 14 due to overbooking despite holding a confirmed ticket
Rebooked on a flight 22 hours later; pre-paid hotel (GBP 320) and theatre booking (GBP 180) were lost as a result
Covered under travel policy TH-TRV-10041; Thai Airways provided a written denied boarding confirmation
Total claim value approx. GBP 500 (~฿22,000); well-documented case
suggested_action: Confirm trip disruption/denied boarding coverage under TH-TRV-10041 and advise the customer to submit the airline's written confirmation plus pre-paid expense receipts for reimbursement.
draft_reply:
Dear Khun Chatree,

What a frustrating experience — I'm sorry this happened to you.

Good news: trip disruption coverage under your travel policy TH-TRV-10041 typically covers pre-paid, non-refundable expenses lost as a direct result of denied boarding when you are not at fault. Your situation appears to meet these criteria.

To process your claim, please submit the following to claims@qoala.co.th quoting policy TH-TRV-10041:
1. Thai Airways' written denied boarding confirmation
2. Hotel booking confirmation and evidence it was non-refundable
3. Theatre booking confirmation and evidence it was non-refundable
4. Your original flight booking and the rebooked flight confirmation

The total claimable amount will be assessed in Thai Baht at the GBP/THB rate on March 14. Reimbursement is typically processed within 10 business days of receiving all documents.

Kind regards,
Qoala Travel Claims Team

Coverage required: accidental death/disability, medical expenses up to ฿100,000 per person, and emergency evacuation

Qoala Group Programmes Team

summary_bullets: Active ransomware attack in progress: company systems encrypted since 6am on March 17 under cyber policy TH-CYB-00031

URGENT — Cyber Incident Response Activated.

Dear Khun Manop / CTO,

I have immediately activated your cyber incident response coverage under policy TH-CYB-00031.

Please take the following actions RIGHT NOW:

1. DO NOT pay the ransom or communicate with the attackers yet — the IR team will advise on this once they assess the situation.
2. DO NOT reboot or wipe any encrypted systems — forensic evidence must be preserved.
3. Isolate all affected systems from the network if not already done.

Our contracted cyber incident response partner is being engaged as we speak. An IR consultant will call your CTO within 15 minutes on the number provided on your chat.

24/7 Cyber Incident Hotline: [number] — Quote policy TH-CYB-00031.

Regarding ransom payment: this is assessed case-by-case by the IR team and may be covered under your policy subject to prior written approval. Do not pay without their sign-off.

We are with you on this.

Qoala Cyber Claims — Emergency Response

summary_bullets: Customer accepted a ฿42,000 home contents settlement (CLM-2026-01882) but now believes they were underpaid by ~฿18,000
Policy states 'replacement cost basis' for electronics; customer was apparently paid on a depreciated value basis instead
Dispute is supported by the policy wording the customer has re-read
Settled claim — customer asking if it is too late to dispute
suggested_action: Review the original settlement calculation for CLM-2026-01882 against the replacement cost basis clause in the policy. If an underpayment is confirmed, issue a supplemental payment. Advise the customer that post-settlement disputes are accepted within the regulatory timeframe.
draft_reply:
Dear Khun Sunee,

Thank you for raising this — you are right to question it and it is not too late to do so.

Post-settlement disputes are accepted within 30 days of the settlement date under Thai insurance regulations, so you are still within the window.

I am pulling claim CLM-2026-01882 and reviewing the settlement calculation against the 'replacement cost basis' clause on page 4 of your policy. If the electronics were indeed settled on a depreciated value rather than replacement cost, this would be an error on our part and we will issue a supplemental payment for the difference.

I will have a full review outcome for you within 3 business days and will contact you directly with the findings.

Thank you for reading your policy carefully — this is exactly the kind of check that ensures fair outcomes.

Kind regards,
Qoala Claims Review Team

Qoala Regulatory Affairs & Legal Team

Qoala Billing Team

summary_bullets: Container with USD 380,000 of electronic components fell ~3 metres during crane loading at Laem Chabang port on March 13
Shipping line MSC has issued a Mate's Receipt noting damage; Bureau Veritas surveyor attending the next day
Marine cargo policy TH-MRN-00421; consignee is a buyer in Germany
Insurer's own surveyor should attend jointly with Bureau Veritas — time-sensitive
suggested_action: Appoint a marine surveyor immediately to attend jointly with Bureau Veritas. Notify the reinsurance team given the USD 380,000 claim value and issue a claim acknowledgement to the insured.
draft_reply:
Dear Khun Rewat,

Thank you for the prompt notification. I have registered this as a priority marine cargo claim under policy TH-MRN-00421.

A Qoala-appointed marine surveyor is being dispatched to Laem Chabang port to attend jointly with the Bureau Veritas surveyor. The surveyor will contact you directly to confirm the attendance time — the aim is to attend today or first thing tomorrow morning.

In the meantime, please:

Qoala Marine Claims Team

summary_bullets: Customer's garden wall was damaged when a neighbour's tree fell during a storm; repair cost approximately ฿15,000
Customer is unsure whether to claim under storm damage or falling objects, or pursue the neighbour directly
Concerned about premium impact of making a claim
Policy TH-HOM-31002 — general coverage query requiring policy interpretation
suggested_action: Confirm coverage under TH-HOM-31002 for storm-related falling object damage, advise on the claims vs no-claims premium impact, and clarify the option of pursuing the neighbour under third-party liability.
draft_reply:
Hi Khun Jintana,

Great question — and the good news is you have a few options here.

1. Your home policy: Damage from a tree falling in a storm typically falls under 'storm damage' or 'falling objects' — both of which are usually covered under standard home insurance. I am confirming the exact wording in policy TH-HOM-31002, but it is likely covered.

2. Pursuing your neighbour: If the tree was dead, diseased, or previously reported as a risk, you may have grounds to claim from your neighbour's home liability coverage. However, if it was a healthy tree felled by an act of nature (storm), this is generally considered an act of God and your neighbour may not be liable.

3. Premium impact: At ฿15,000, it is worth checking your policy excess first. If your excess is, say, ฿10,000, you would only recover ฿5,000 and making a claim could affect your renewal premium. I can check your excess and give you a clearer picture.

I'll come back to you with the specific policy details within 24 hours so you can make an informed decision.

Kind regards,
Qoala Home Team

summary_bullets: Tuk-tuk tour operator adding 3 new vehicles to fleet policy TH-FLT-00881 with Bangkok plates for tourist operations in Rattanakosin
Chassis numbers and vehicle codes provided; interim cover notes needed before Monday operations begin
Case is marked Done — endorsement and cover notes likely issued

summary_bullets: Customer has just received a confirmed Stage 2 breast cancer diagnosis and holds a critical illness rider on life policy TH-LIF-29801
She is distressed and unsure of the process; treatment needs to begin soon and she needs financial support urgently
Asking to confirm coverage, required documents, and payment timeline
Highly sensitive situation requiring empathetic, clear, and fast response
suggested_action: Confirm critical illness coverage for breast cancer under TH-LIF-29801, provide a simplified claims document checklist, and assign a dedicated case manager to support the customer through the process with care.
draft_reply:
Dear Khun Chanida,

Thank you for reaching out to us, and please know that we are here for you during this time.

First — yes, Stage 2 breast cancer is covered under the critical illness rider attached to your life policy TH-LIF-29801. You are entitled to a lump-sum benefit and we want to make this process as simple and quick as possible for you.

Here is what you need to provide:
1. Doctor's written confirmation of diagnosis with pathology report
2. Hospital diagnosis certificate
3. A completed critical illness claim form (I am attaching this now)
4. A copy of your national ID

Once all documents are received, we target a decision within 7 business days and payment within 3 business days of approval.

I am assigning you a dedicated case manager — Khun [Name] — who will call you within 24 hours and guide you personally through every step. You will not have to navigate this alone.

Please take good care of yourself. We will take care of the claim.

With warm regards,
Qoala Life & Health Claims Team

summary_bullets: Worker Arthon Saelim (age 34) fell from scaffolding at a Thonburi construction site on March 15, sustaining a fractured pelvis
Currently in Siriraj Hospital ICU; stable but requires surgery and extended rehabilitation
Workers' compensation policy TH-WKC-00302; employer asking about claims form, wage replacement, medical limits, and Department of Labour notification
Serious workplace injury with regulatory notification obligations — multi-department action required
suggested_action: Acknowledge the claim urgently, provide the claims notification form, confirm weekly wage replacement and medical coverage limits under TH-WKC-00302, and advise on mandatory Department of Labour notification requirements.
draft_reply:
Dear Khun Kriangkrai,

Thank you for notifying us and I hope Mr. Arthon makes a full recovery.

I have registered an urgent workers' compensation claim under policy TH-WKC-00302. Here are the answers to your questions:

1. Claims notification form: attached to this email. Please complete and return within 48 hours.
2. Wage replacement: Yes, weekly wage replacement benefit applies from the day following the incident, subject to medical confirmation of inability to work. Rate is [X]% of daily wage per the policy schedule.
3. Medical expenses: Covered up to the medical expenses limit in your policy schedule. Siriraj Hospital is a government hospital and expenses will be covered directly — please ensure all invoices are submitted to us.
4. Department of Labour notification: Yes, under Thai Labour Protection Act, a serious workplace injury (hospitalisation) must be notified to the Department of Labour Protection and Welfare within 15 days. We recommend doing this immediately.

A dedicated claims handler will contact you by end of day.

Sincerely,
Qoala Workers' Compensation Claims Team

summary_bullets: Unsolicited industry newsletter from insurancenews.co.th with OIC regulatory updates and market trend summaries
Not from a customer; not a spam or phishing attempt — just an unwanted subscription email
No action required on any insurance matter; sender provides an unsubscribe option
Low priority, no customer impact
suggested_action: Unsubscribe from the mailing list and archive. No customer reply needed.

summary_bullets: Retail store at MBK Center (Unit 3F-42) forcibly closed since March 15 due to fire in adjacent unit — not directly fire-damaged but affected by smoke and mandatory closure order
Revenue loss estimated at ฿85,000 per day; business interruption policy TH-BIN-00501 held
Customer asking whether forced closure due to neighbouring damage triggers BI coverage
Likely covered under 'denial of access' or 'adjacent premises' BI extension — needs verification
suggested_action: Urgently review the BI extension clauses in TH-BIN-00501 for 'denial of access' or 'adjacent premises' triggers. If confirmed covered, assign a loss adjuster and begin loss quantification given the ฿85,000/day exposure.
draft_reply:
Dear Khun Benjamas,

Thank you for notifying us promptly. I understand how serious this disruption is with the revenue impact accumulating daily.

I am urgently reviewing policy TH-BIN-00501, specifically the business interruption extension clauses. Forced closure by authorities due to damage to adjacent premises — where your own property has not been directly damaged by fire — is typically addressed under a 'denial of access' or 'adjacent premises' extension. Smoke damage to inventory may also be a separate covered loss.

I will confirm coverage within 4 hours. In the meantime, please:
1. Obtain and preserve the mall management's written closure order
2. Begin keeping a daily record of lost revenue and fixed costs (rent, utilities, staff salaries) that continue during closure
3. Document the smoke damage to inventory with photos and an estimated value list

A loss adjuster will be assigned today to begin quantifying the business interruption loss.

Kind regards,
Qoala Commercial Claims Team

summary_bullets: Recently married customer wants to update beneficiary on life policy TH-LIF-18820 from his mother to his wife Nisa Chaiyo
Wife's national ID number partially provided; asking for the process and required documents
Straightforward beneficiary change endorsement — no premium impact
Customer also wants an updated policy schedule once processed
suggested_action: Send the beneficiary change form to the customer, process the update once signed documentation is received, and issue an updated policy schedule.
draft_reply:
Dear Khun Pisanu,

Congratulations on your marriage!

Updating your beneficiary on policy TH-LIF-18820 is a simple process. Here is what you need to do:

1. Complete the attached Beneficiary Change Request form
2. Sign the form (witness signature is not required for this policy type)
3. Attach a copy of your wife's national ID (Khun Nisa Chaiyo) and your marriage certificate
4. Return to us by email at policy.changes@qoala.co.th

Your wife does not need to sign the form, though she will need to provide her ID as listed above.

Once we receive the documents, the change will be processed within 3 business days and an updated policy schedule confirming Khun Nisa as the new beneficiary will be sent to your registered email.

Warm regards,
Qoala Life Policy Services Team
